titleEnglish: Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field
description: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13, Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga, presents a profound discourse on the distinction between the body (Kshetra or field) and the soul (Kshetrajna or knower of the field). Lord Krishna explains to Arjuna that this body is called the field, and the one who knows it is the knower of the field. He enumerates twenty virtues that constitute true knowledge, including humility, non-violence, patience, and devotion. Krishna describes the Supreme Brahman as beginningless, beyond existence and non-existence, with hands, feet, eyes, and heads everywhere. He explains how Prakriti (nature) and Purusha (consciousness) are both beginningless, and that all modifications and qualities arise from Prakriti. The chapter culminates in the teaching that one who perceives the difference between the field and its knower through the eye of wisdom attains liberation.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
अर्जुन उवाच —
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च।
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव॥१॥
verses.transliteration:
Arjuna Uvacha —
Prakritim Purusham Chaiva Kshetram Kshetrajnam-Eva Cha.
Etad-Veditum-Ichchhami Jnanam Jneyam Cha Keshava.
verses.hindiTranslation: अर्जुन ने कहा — हे केशव! प्रकृति और पुरुष, क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ, ज्ञान और ज्ञेय — इन सबको मैं जानना चाहता हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Arjuna said — O Keshava, I wish to know about Prakriti (nature) and Purusha (the conscious being), the field and the knower of the field, and about knowledge and the object of knowledge.
verses.commentary: Arjuna asks six fundamental questions covering the entire spectrum of metaphysical inquiry. This verse sets the stage for Krishna's discourse on the distinction between matter and spirit. In some recensions of the Gita, this verse is not included, and the chapter begins directly with Krishna's response.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः॥२॥
verses.transliteration:
Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Idam Shariram Kaunteya Kshetram-Ity-Abhidhiyate.
Etad-Yo Vetti Tam Prahuh Kshetrajna Iti Tad-Vidah.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान ने कहा — हे कौन्तेय! यह शरीर 'क्षेत्र' कहा जाता है और जो इसे जानता है उसे ज्ञानीजन 'क्षेत्रज्ञ' कहते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: The Blessed Lord said — This body, O son of Kunti, is called the field, and the one who knows it is called the knower of the field by the wise.
verses.commentary: Krishna begins by defining the two fundamental categories. The body is the field where actions are performed and their results experienced, while the soul is the conscious witness who knows the field. This distinction between the seen and the seer is central to Vedantic philosophy.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम॥३॥
verses.transliteration:
Kshetrajnam Cha-Api Mam Viddhi Sarva-Kshetreshu Bharata.
Kshetra-Kshetrajnayor-Jnanam Yat-Taj-Jnanam Matam Mama.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे भारत! सब क्षेत्रों (शरीरों) में क्षेत्रज्ञ मुझे ही जानो। क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ का जो ज्ञान है — वही मेरे मत में सच्चा ज्ञान है।
verses.englishTranslation: Know Me also as the knower of the field in all fields, O Bharata. The knowledge of the field and the knower of the field — that I consider to be true knowledge.
verses.commentary: This is one of the most important verses in the Gita. Krishna declares Himself as the supreme knower present in all bodies, establishing the identity of the individual soul with the Supreme. True knowledge is not mere intellectual understanding but the direct realization of the distinction between matter and spirit.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।
स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे शृणु॥४॥
verses.transliteration:
Tat-Kshetram Yach-Cha Yadrik-Cha Yad-Vikari Yatash-Cha Yat.
Sa Cha Yo Yat-Prabhavash-Cha Tat-Samasena Me Shrinu.
verses.hindiTranslation: वह क्षेत्र क्या है, कैसा है, किन विकारों वाला है, किससे उत्पन्न है, और वह क्षेत्रज्ञ कौन है तथा उसका क्या प्रभाव है — यह सब संक्षेप में मुझसे सुनो।
verses.englishTranslation: What that field is, what its nature is, what its modifications are, from where it arises, who the knower is and what his powers are — hear all this from Me in brief.
verses.commentary: Krishna promises to explain the complete philosophy of the field and its knower in a concise manner. He will cover the nature, modifications, and origin of the field (body-mind complex) as well as the identity and powers of the knower (the conscious self). This sets the agenda for the rest of the chapter.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक्।
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः॥५॥
verses.transliteration:
Rishibhir-Bahudha Gitam Chhandobhir-Vividhaih Prithak.
Brahma-Sutra-Padaish-Chaiva Hetumad-Bhir-Vinishchitaih.

wordByWord.meaning: by the aphorisms of the Brahma Sutras

verses.hindiTranslation: इस क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञ तत्त्व को ऋषियों ने अनेक प्रकार से गाया है, विविध वैदिक छन्दों में अलग-अलग कहा है, और ब्रह्मसूत्र के युक्तिपूर्ण निश्चित पदों द्वारा भी प्रतिपादित किया है।
verses.englishTranslation: This knowledge of the field and its knower has been sung by sages in many ways, in various Vedic hymns distinctly, and also through the well-reasoned and conclusive aphorisms of the Brahma Sutras.
verses.commentary: Krishna validates this teaching by citing its scriptural authority. The Rishis have described this truth through Vedic hymns and the Brahma Sutras, which present logical arguments for the nature of Brahman. This shows that the Gita's teaching is not new but is rooted in the ancient Vedic tradition.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः॥६॥
verses.transliteration:
Maha-Bhutany-Ahankaro Buddhir-Avyaktam-Eva Cha.
Indriyani Dasha-Ekam Cha Pancha Cha-Indriya-Gocharah.
verses.hindiTranslation: पाँच महाभूत, अहंकार, बुद्धि, अव्यक्त प्रकृति, दस इन्द्रियाँ, एक मन और पाँच इन्द्रियों के विषय —
verses.englishTranslation: The five great elements, ego, intellect, the unmanifest Prakriti, the ten senses and the mind, and the five objects of the senses —
verses.commentary: Krishna begins enumerating the components of the field (Kshetra). The five great elements are earth, water, fire, air, and space. The ten senses are five organs of perception (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch) and five organs of action (speech, hands, feet, reproduction, excretion), plus the mind as the eleventh. This verse continues into the next.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृतिः।
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम्॥७॥
verses.transliteration:
Ichchha Dveshah Sukham Duhkham Sanghatash-Chetana Dhritih.
Etat-Kshetram Samasena Sa-Vikaram-Udahritam.
verses.hindiTranslation: इच्छा, द्वेष, सुख, दुःख, शरीर का संघात, चेतना और धृति — इस प्रकार यह क्षेत्र अपने विकारों सहित संक्षेप में वर्णित किया गया।
verses.englishTranslation: Desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the physical aggregate, consciousness, and fortitude — this, in brief, has been described as the field with its modifications.
verses.commentary: Completing the description of the field, Krishna includes psychological aspects like desire, aversion, pleasure, and pain. The field is not just the physical body but includes the entire psycho-physical apparatus with all its modifications. Understanding all these as part of the field helps the seeker to dis-identify from them.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम्।
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः॥८॥
verses.transliteration:
Amanitvam-Adambhitvam-Ahimsa Kshantir-Arjavam.
Acharyo-Upasanam Shaucham Sthairyam-Atma-Vinigrahah.
verses.hindiTranslation: विनम्रता, दम्भहीनता, अहिंसा, क्षमा, सरलता, गुरुसेवा, शुद्धि, स्थिरता और आत्मसंयम —
verses.englishTranslation: Humility, absence of hypocrisy, non-violence, patience, straightforwardness, service to the teacher, purity, steadfastness, and self-control —
verses.commentary: Krishna now begins listing the twenty qualities that constitute true knowledge (Jnana). These are not mere intellectual concepts but lived virtues that prepare the mind for spiritual realization. The first set includes moral and ethical qualities that form the foundation of spiritual life.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च।
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम्॥९॥
verses.transliteration:
Indriya-Artheshu Vairagyam-Anahankara Eva Cha.
Janma-Mrityu-Jara-Vyadhi-Duhkha-Dosha-Anudarshanam.
verses.hindiTranslation: इन्द्रिय-विषयों में वैराग्य, अहंकार का अभाव, और जन्म, मृत्यु, जरा, व्याधि तथा दुःख में दोष का बार-बार दर्शन —
verses.englishTranslation: Dispassion towards sense objects, absence of ego, and constant perception of the evil and suffering inherent in birth, death, old age, and disease —
verses.commentary: The seeker must cultivate detachment from sensory pleasures and dissolve the false ego. Repeatedly contemplating the sufferings inherent in the cycle of birth, death, old age, and disease develops the dispassion needed for spiritual progress. This is not pessimism but clear-sighted wisdom.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु।
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु॥१०॥
verses.transliteration:
Asaktir-Anabhishvangah Putra-Dara-Griha-Adishu.
Nityam Cha Sama-Chittatvam-Ishta-Anishta-Upapattishu.
verses.hindiTranslation: पुत्र, पत्नी, घर आदि में आसक्ति और ममता का अभाव, तथा इष्ट और अनिष्ट की प्राप्ति में सदा समान चित्तता —
verses.englishTranslation: Non-attachment and absence of excessive affection for son, wife, home and the like, and constant equanimity of mind upon the occurrence of desirable and undesirable events —
verses.commentary: Non-attachment does not mean abandoning one's family but performing one's duties without clinging or possessiveness. Equanimity in favorable and unfavorable circumstances reflects a mind grounded in wisdom. This balanced state allows one to respond to life's events without being overwhelmed by them.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी।
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि॥११॥
verses.transliteration:
Mayi Cha-Ananya-Yogena Bhaktir-Avyabhicharini.
Vivikta-Desha-Sevitvam-Aratir-Jana-Samsadi.
verses.hindiTranslation: मुझमें अनन्य योग द्वारा अव्यभिचारिणी भक्ति, एकान्त स्थान का सेवन, और जनसमूह में अरुचि —
verses.englishTranslation: Unwavering devotion to Me through exclusive yoga, preference for solitary places, and absence of delight in the company of worldly people —
verses.commentary: Devotion to God is listed among the qualities of knowledge, showing that jnana and bhakti are not opposed but complementary. Seeking solitude is recommended not out of misanthropy but to create conditions conducive to contemplation. A quiet environment helps the aspirant maintain focus on the spiritual path.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम्।
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा॥१२॥
verses.transliteration:
Adhyatma-Jnana-Nityatvam Tattva-Jnana-Artha-Darshanam.
Etaj-Jnanam-Iti Proktam-Ajnanam Yad-Ato'nyatha.

wordByWord.meaning: perception of the goal of true knowledge

verses.hindiTranslation: अध्यात्मज्ञान में नित्य स्थिति और तत्त्वज्ञान के प्रयोजन का दर्शन — यह सब 'ज्ञान' कहा गया है और जो इससे विपरीत है वह 'अज्ञान' है।
verses.englishTranslation: Constancy in self-knowledge and perception of the goal of true knowledge — all this is declared to be knowledge, and what is contrary to this is ignorance.
verses.commentary: Krishna concludes the list of twenty virtues by emphasizing constant abidance in spiritual knowledge and keeping the goal of liberation always in view. These qualities collectively purify the mind and prepare it for the direct realization of the Self. Anything contrary to these virtues is termed ignorance.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते।
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते॥१३॥
verses.transliteration:
Jneyam Yat-Tat Pravakshyami Yaj-Jnatva-Amritam-Ashnute.
Anadi-Mat-Param Brahma Na Sat-Tan-Na-Asad-Uchyate.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो जानने योग्य है उसे मैं बताता हूँ, जिसे जानकर मनुष्य अमृत प्राप्त करता है — वह अनादि परब्रह्म न सत् कहा जाता है, न असत्।
verses.englishTranslation: I shall declare that which is to be known, knowing which one attains immortality — the beginningless Supreme Brahman, which is said to be neither existent nor non-existent.
verses.commentary: Krishna now describes the Knowable — the Supreme Brahman. It is beginningless, meaning it has no origin and is eternal. It transcends the categories of existence and non-existence as understood by the limited human mind. Knowing this Brahman directly leads to immortality and freedom from the cycle of birth and death.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति॥१४॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarvatah Pani-Padam Tat Sarvato'kshi-Shiro-Mukham.
Sarvatah Shrutimat-Loke Sarvam-Avritya Tishthati.
verses.hindiTranslation: उस ब्रह्म के सब ओर हाथ-पाँव हैं, सब ओर नेत्र, सिर और मुख हैं, सब ओर कान हैं — वह इस संसार में सबको व्याप्त करके स्थित है।
verses.englishTranslation: With hands and feet everywhere, eyes, heads, and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere — That exists in the world, pervading everything.
verses.commentary: This verse echoes the Purusha Sukta of the Rig Veda and the Shvetashvatara Upanishad. Brahman is described as omnipresent and all-pervading, functioning through every hand, seeing through every eye, and hearing through every ear in creation. This is not a physical description but points to the universal consciousness present in all beings.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम्।
असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च॥१५॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarva-Indriya-Guna-Abhasam Sarva-Indriya-Vivarjitam.
Asaktam Sarva-Bhrich-Chaiva Nirgunam Guna-Bhoktri Cha.
verses.hindiTranslation: वह सब इन्द्रियों के गुणों को प्रकाशित करने वाला है, फिर भी सब इन्द्रियों से रहित है। वह अनासक्त है फिर भी सबका भरण-पोषण करता है। वह निर्गुण है फिर भी गुणों का भोक्ता है।
verses.englishTranslation: It illuminates the functions of all the senses, yet is devoid of all senses. It is unattached yet sustains all. It is without qualities yet is the experiencer of all qualities.
verses.commentary: This verse presents a series of beautiful paradoxes about Brahman. It functions through the senses yet has no senses itself. It supports all creation yet remains unattached. It is beyond the three Gunas yet experiences them. These apparent contradictions point to the transcendent nature of the Absolute which cannot be captured by logical categories.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च।
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत्॥१६॥
verses.transliteration:
Bahir-Antash-Cha Bhutanam-Acharam Charam-Eva Cha.
Suksshmatvat-Tad-Avijneyam Durastham Cha-Antike Cha Tat.
verses.hindiTranslation: वह चराचर सब भूतों के भीतर और बाहर है। वह अचल भी है और चल भी। वह सूक्ष्म होने से अविज्ञेय है। वह बहुत दूर है और बहुत निकट भी है।
verses.englishTranslation: It exists outside and inside all beings, is both the moving and the unmoving. Due to its subtlety, it is incomprehensible. It is far away and yet very near.
verses.commentary: Brahman pervades all of creation — it is both within and without every being. It is simultaneously the static ground of all existence and the dynamic principle behind all movement. Its extreme subtlety makes it imperceptible to the senses, yet it is the closest reality — nearer than one's own self.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम्।
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च॥१७॥
verses.transliteration:
Avibhaktam Cha Bhuteshu Vibhaktam-Iva Cha Sthitam.
Bhuta-Bhartri Cha Taj-Jneyam Grasishnu Prabhavishnu Cha.
verses.hindiTranslation: वह ज्ञेय ब्रह्म भूतों में विभक्त सा प्रतीत होता हुआ भी अविभक्त है। वह भूतों का भरण-पोषण करने वाला, संहार करने वाला और उत्पन्न करने वाला है।
verses.englishTranslation: Though undivided, it appears as if divided among beings. It is to be known as the sustainer of all beings, and also their devourer and creator.
verses.commentary: Brahman is one and indivisible, yet it appears to be many when manifested in different beings — just as space appears divided when enclosed in different pots. It performs the triple function of creation, sustenance, and dissolution. Understanding this unified reality behind apparent diversity is the essence of spiritual knowledge.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते।
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम्॥१८॥
verses.transliteration:
Jyotisham-Api Taj-Jyotis-Tamasah Param-Uchyate.
Jnanam Jneyam Jnana-Gamyam Hridi Sarvasya Vishthitam.
verses.hindiTranslation: वह ज्योतियों की भी ज्योति है और अन्धकार से परे कहा जाता है। वह ज्ञानस्वरूप, ज्ञेय और ज्ञान द्वारा प्राप्य है — सबके हृदय में विराजमान है।
verses.englishTranslation: That is the light of all lights, said to be beyond darkness. It is knowledge itself, the object of knowledge, and attainable through knowledge — seated in the hearts of all.
verses.commentary: One of the most luminous verses in the Gita, describing Brahman as the ultimate source of all light — the sun, moon, and fire shine only because of It. It transcends all darkness of ignorance. It is simultaneously the means of knowing (knowledge), the thing to be known (the knowable), and the goal reached through knowledge, residing in every heart.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः।
मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते॥१९॥
verses.transliteration:
Iti Kshetram Tatha Jnanam Jneyam Choktam Samasatah.
Mad-Bhakta Etad-Vijnaya Mad-Bhavayo-Papadyate.
verses.hindiTranslation: इस प्रकार क्षेत्र, ज्ञान और ज्ञेय का संक्षेप में वर्णन किया गया। मेरा भक्त इसे समझकर मेरे स्वरूप को प्राप्त होता है।
verses.englishTranslation: Thus the field, knowledge, and the knowable have been briefly stated. My devotee, understanding this, becomes worthy of attaining My nature.
verses.commentary: Krishna summarizes the three topics covered so far — the field (body-mind), knowledge (the twenty virtues), and the knowable (Brahman). The one who understands these through devotion attains the divine nature of Krishna. This shows that intellectual understanding must be combined with devotion for complete realization.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि।
विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान्॥२०॥
verses.transliteration:
Prakritim Purusham Chaiva Viddhy-Anadi Ubhav-Api.
Vikaraamsh-Cha Gunaamsh-Chaiva Viddhi Prakriti-Sambhavan.
verses.hindiTranslation: प्रकृति और पुरुष — इन दोनों को अनादि जानो। और विकारों तथा गुणों को प्रकृति से उत्पन्न जानो।
verses.englishTranslation: Know that both Prakriti (nature) and Purusha (the conscious being) are beginningless. And know that all modifications and qualities are born of Prakriti.
verses.commentary: Both Prakriti (material nature) and Purusha (consciousness) are eternal and beginningless. However, all modifications of form and all three Gunas (Sattva, Rajas, Tamas) arise from Prakriti alone. Purusha, being pure consciousness, is free from all modifications. This Sankhya analysis helps the seeker understand what belongs to nature and what belongs to the Self.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते।
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते॥२१॥
verses.transliteration:
Karya-Karana-Kartritve Hetuh Prakritir-Uchyate.
Purushah Sukha-Duhkhanam Bhoktritve Hetur-Uchyate.
verses.hindiTranslation: कार्य (शरीर) और करण (इन्द्रिय) की उत्पत्ति में प्रकृति हेतु कही जाती है। सुख-दुःख के भोगने में पुरुष हेतु कहा जाता है।
verses.englishTranslation: Prakriti is said to be the cause in the production of the body and the senses. The Purusha is said to be the cause in the experience of pleasure and pain.
verses.commentary: Prakriti is responsible for the physical aspects — the body and its sense organs. Purusha, the conscious self, is responsible for the subjective experience of pleasure and pain. Neither Prakriti alone nor Purusha alone can produce experience; it is their conjunction that creates the entire drama of worldly life.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान्।
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु॥२२॥
verses.transliteration:
Purushah Prakriti-Stho Hi Bhunkte Prakriti-Jan-Gunan.
Karanam Guna-Sango'sya Sad-Asad-Yoni-Janmasu.
verses.hindiTranslation: प्रकृति में स्थित पुरुष प्रकृति से उत्पन्न गुणों का भोग करता है। इन गुणों का संग ही इसके अच्छी और बुरी योनियों में जन्म का कारण है।
verses.englishTranslation: The Purusha, seated in Prakriti, experiences the qualities born of Prakriti. Attachment to these qualities is the cause of its birth in good and evil wombs.
verses.commentary: The soul, though pure and eternal, becomes entangled in worldly experience by identifying with the body and its qualities. This attachment to the Gunas determines the quality of future births — association with Sattva leads to higher births, Rajas to middle, and Tamas to lower births. Liberation comes from breaking this attachment through discrimination.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः।
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः॥२३॥
verses.transliteration:
Upadrashta-Anumanta Cha Bharta Bhokta Maheshvarah.
Parama-Atme-Ti Cha-Apy-Ukto Dehe'smin Purushah Parah.
verses.hindiTranslation: इस देह में स्थित परम पुरुष साक्षी, अनुमन्ता, भरण-पोषण करने वाला, भोक्ता, महान ईश्वर और परमात्मा भी कहा गया है।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Purusha in this body is called the witness, the permitter, the sustainer, the experiencer, the great Lord, and also the Supreme Self (Paramatma).
verses.commentary: Krishna describes the Supreme Self dwelling in every body using six powerful names. As witness, He observes all; as permitter, He sanctions all actions; as sustainer, He maintains life; as experiencer, He is the ultimate enjoyer; as Maheshvara, He is the great Lord; and as Paramatma, He is the Supreme Self. These names reveal the multifaceted relationship between God and the individual.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते॥२४॥
verses.transliteration:
Ya Evam Vetti Purusham Prakritim Cha Gunaih Saha.
Sarvatha Vartamano'pi Na Sa Bhuyo'bhijayate.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो इस प्रकार पुरुष को और गुणों सहित प्रकृति को जान लेता है, वह सब प्रकार से स्थित होता हुआ भी फिर जन्म नहीं लेता।
verses.englishTranslation: He who thus knows the Purusha and Prakriti along with its qualities, in whatever condition he may be, is not born again.
verses.commentary: This verse gives the liberating promise — whoever truly understands the distinction between Purusha and Prakriti, and realizes that all qualities belong to Prakriti alone, is freed from the cycle of rebirth regardless of their current situation. This knowledge is not theoretical but experiential, arising from deep meditation and discrimination.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना।
अन्ये साङ्ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे॥२५॥
verses.transliteration:
Dhyanena-Atmani Pashyanti Kechid-Atmanam-Atmana.
Anye Sankhyena Yogena Karma-Yogena Cha-Apare.
verses.hindiTranslation: कुछ लोग ध्यान द्वारा आत्मा में आत्मा को देखते हैं, कुछ सांख्य योग से और कुछ कर्मयोग से।
verses.englishTranslation: Some perceive the Self in the self through meditation, others through the path of knowledge (Sankhya Yoga), and yet others through the path of action (Karma Yoga).
verses.commentary: Krishna acknowledges that there are multiple valid paths to Self-realization. Some attain it through meditation (Dhyana Yoga), some through philosophical discrimination (Sankhya/Jnana Yoga), and others through selfless action (Karma Yoga). This shows the Gita's inclusive approach, accommodating seekers of different temperaments.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते।
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः॥२६॥
verses.transliteration:
Anye Tv-Evam-Ajanantah Shrutva-Anyebhya Upasate.
Te'pi Cha-Atitaranty-Eva Mrityum Shruti-Parayanah.
verses.hindiTranslation: और कुछ लोग, जो इन योगों को नहीं जानते, दूसरों से सुनकर उपासना करते हैं — वे भी श्रवण-परायण होकर मृत्यु को अवश्य पार कर जाते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Yet others, not knowing these paths, hear from others and worship accordingly. They too, devoted to what they have heard, certainly cross beyond death.
verses.commentary: Even those who lack the capacity for independent meditation or philosophical inquiry can attain liberation through faithful hearing and practice. This compassionate verse ensures that no sincere seeker is excluded from the path. Shraddha (faith) in the teachings heard from a guru is itself a powerful means of liberation.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम्।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ॥२७॥
verses.transliteration:
Yavat-Sanjayate Kinchit-Sattvam Sthavara-Jangamam.
Kshetra-Kshetrajna-Samyogat-Tad-Viddhi Bharatarshabha.

wordByWord.meaning: from the union of field and knower

verses.hindiTranslation: हे भरतश्रेष्ठ! जो कुछ भी — स्थावर या जंगम — उत्पन्न होता है, उसे क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ के संयोग से उत्पन्न जानो।
verses.englishTranslation: Whatever being is born, whether moving or unmoving, know that it arises from the union of the field and the knower of the field, O best of the Bharatas.
verses.commentary: All creation, whether animate or inanimate, results from the conjunction of matter (Kshetra) and consciousness (Kshetrajna). Without matter, consciousness has no medium of expression; without consciousness, matter remains inert. This verse summarizes the Sankhya cosmology that underlies the Gita's philosophy.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्।
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति॥२८॥
verses.transliteration:
Samam Sarveshu Bhuteshu Tishthantam Parameshvaram.
Vinashyatsv-Avinashyantam Yah Pashyati Sa Pashyati.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो सब भूतों में समान रूप से स्थित परमेश्वर को — नाशवान में अविनाशी को — देखता है, वही सच में देखता है।
verses.englishTranslation: He who sees the Supreme Lord dwelling equally in all beings, the imperishable within the perishable — he alone truly sees.
verses.commentary: This is the vision of the enlightened — seeing the one unchanging Lord equally present in all beings while their bodies perish. True seeing is not physical sight but spiritual insight. The emphasis 'he truly sees' implies that without this vision, all other seeing is merely partial or illusory.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम्।
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम्॥२९॥
verses.transliteration:
Samam Pashyan Hi Sarvatra Samavasthitam-Ishvaram.
Na Hinasty-Atmana-Atmanam Tato Yati Param Gatim.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो सब जगह समान रूप से स्थित ईश्वर को देखता हुआ आत्मा द्वारा आत्मा को नष्ट नहीं करता, वह परम गति को प्राप्त होता है।
verses.englishTranslation: Seeing the Lord equally present everywhere, he does not destroy the Self by the self, and thus attains the supreme goal.
verses.commentary: When one sees God equally in all beings, one naturally refrains from harming others, for harming another would be harming the same Lord who dwells within. By not degrading one's own spiritual nature through wrong actions, such a person reaches the highest state. This verse connects the vision of equality with ethical conduct and spiritual attainment.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः।
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति॥३०॥
verses.transliteration:
Prakritya-Eva Cha Karmani Kriyamanani Sarvashah.
Yah Pashyati Tatha-Atmanam-Akartaram Sa Pashyati.
verses.hindiTranslation: सब कर्मों को प्रकृति द्वारा ही सम्पादित होते हुए देखता है और आत्मा को अकर्ता देखता है — वही सच में देखता है।
verses.englishTranslation: He who sees that all actions are performed by Prakriti alone and that the Self is the non-doer — he alone truly sees.
verses.commentary: The enlightened person recognizes that all physical and mental activities are functions of Prakriti (material nature), while the Self (Atman) remains a non-participating witness. This understanding frees one from the bondage of karma. When one no longer identifies as the doer, the chain of action and reaction ceases to bind.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति।
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा॥३१॥
verses.transliteration:
Yada Bhuta-Prithag-Bhavam-Ekastham-Anupashyati.
Tata Eva Cha Vistaram Brahma Sampadyate Tada.
verses.hindiTranslation: जब साधक भूतों के पृथक् भाव को एक में स्थित देखता है और उसी से उनका विस्तार हुआ जानता है, तब वह ब्रह्म को प्राप्त होता है।
verses.englishTranslation: When one perceives the diverse existence of beings as rooted in the One, and their expansion as emanating from That alone, then one attains Brahman.
verses.commentary: This verse describes the moment of spiritual awakening when the apparent diversity of creation is seen as emanating from a single source. Just as many waves arise from one ocean, all beings emerge from one Brahman. This unitive vision, where multiplicity is seen as the expression of unity, marks the attainment of Brahman-realization.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः।
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते॥३२॥
verses.transliteration:
Anaditvat-Nirgunatvat-Paramatma-Ayam-Avyayah.
Sharira-Stho'pi Kaunteya Na Karoti Na Lipyate.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे कौन्तेय! यह अव्यय परमात्मा अनादि और निर्गुण होने से शरीर में स्थित होकर भी न कुछ करता है, न लिप्त होता है।
verses.englishTranslation: Being beginningless and without qualities, this imperishable Supreme Self, though dwelling in the body, O son of Kunti, neither acts nor is tainted.
verses.commentary: The Supreme Self is untouched by action or its consequences despite residing in the body. Being beginningless, it was never created and so cannot be affected by anything. Being Nirguna (without material qualities), the actions of the Gunas cannot stain it. Like space contained in a pot that remains unaffected by what happens inside the pot, the Self remains ever pure.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते।
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते॥३३॥
verses.transliteration:
Yatha Sarvagatam Saukshymyad-Akasham No-Palipyate.
Sarvatra-Avasthito Dehe Tatha-Atma No-Palipyate.
verses.hindiTranslation: जैसे सर्वव्यापी आकाश सूक्ष्म होने से किसी से लिप्त नहीं होता, वैसे ही सब जगह शरीर में स्थित आत्मा लिप्त नहीं होता।
verses.englishTranslation: Just as the all-pervading space is not tainted because of its subtlety, so the Self, though present everywhere in the body, is not tainted.
verses.commentary: Krishna uses the beautiful analogy of space (Akasha) to explain the purity of the Self. Space pervades everything — it is inside a jar of perfume, inside refuse, inside a temple — yet it is never contaminated by what it contains. Similarly, the Self pervades the body and all its activities but remains eternally pure and untouched.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः।
क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत॥३४॥
verses.transliteration:
Yatha Prakashayaty-Ekah Kritsnam Lokam-Imam Ravih.
Kshetram Kshetri Tatha Kritsnam Prakashayati Bharata.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे भारत! जैसे एक सूर्य इस समस्त लोक को प्रकाशित करता है, वैसे ही एक क्षेत्री (आत्मा) समस्त क्षेत्र (शरीर) को प्रकाशित करता है।
verses.englishTranslation: Just as the one sun illuminates this entire world, so does the one knower of the field (the Self) illuminate the entire field (the body), O Bharata.
verses.commentary: This is one of the Gita's most powerful analogies. Just as one sun illuminates the whole world without being affected by what it illumines, the one conscious Self illuminates the entire body-mind complex without being tainted by its activities. The sun does not become wet by illuminating water or dirty by illuminating mud — similarly, the Self remains pure while making all experience possible.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा।
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम्॥३५॥
verses.transliteration:
Kshetra-Kshetrajnayor-Evam-Antaram Jnana-Chakshsusha.
Bhuta-Prakriti-Moksham Cha Ye Vidur-Yanti Te Param.
verses.hindiTranslation: इस प्रकार ज्ञान-नेत्रों से क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ का अन्तर तथा भूतप्रकृति से मोक्ष का मार्ग जो जानते हैं, वे परम गति को प्राप्त होते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Those who, with the eye of knowledge, thus perceive the difference between the field and the knower of the field, and the means of liberation from material nature — they attain the Supreme.
verses.commentary: The concluding verse summarizes the entire chapter's teaching. Those who develop the eye of knowledge — the ability to discriminate between the body (field) and the Self (knower) — and understand how to free themselves from the bondage of Prakriti, attain the Supreme. This knowledge is not mere intellectual understanding but a transformative vision that liberates.
